executiveSummary.summary: Strong market need with differentiated approach to rural telehealth challenges. Your Epic background and NIH funding provide credibility. However, regulatory complexity, slow adoption cycles, and specialist recruitment present significant execution risks that require careful sequencing.

marketSize.tam: $45B US telehealth market, with rural representing ~20% or $9B
marketSize.sam: $2.8B rural telehealth market (60M rural Americans × $47 avg annual telehealth spend)
marketSize.som: $15-30M realistic 3-year market (500-1000 rural clinics × $24K annual revenue per clinic)
marketSize.methodology: Based on rural population census data, current telehealth penetration rates, and clinic density in rural areas

competitors.name: Teladoc Health
competitors.description: Largest telehealth platform with B2B2C model
competitors.strength: Scale, established payer relationships, broad specialist network

competitors.name: Amwell
competitors.strength: Strong EHR partnerships, enterprise sales experience
competitors.weakness: Not optimized for low-bandwidth or rural workflows

competitors.name: MDLive
competitors.weakness: Limited rural focus, no clinic integration strategy

competitors.name: Doxy.me
competitors.strength: Easy to use, no downloads required, popular with small practices
competitors.weakness: Limited EHR integration, no rural-specific features

competitors.name: Project ECHO
competitors.description: Hub-and-spoke model for specialist knowledge sharing
competitors.weakness: Educational focus, not direct patient care platform

marketAnalysis.positioning: The only telehealth platform purpose-built for rural healthcare delivery challenges
marketAnalysis.timing: Perfect timing with permanent telehealth reimbursement expansions post-COVID and $2B in rural health funding from Infrastructure Act

valueProposition.coreProblem: Rural patients can't access specialists due to distance and rural clinics can't retain specialists due to economics
valueProposition.targetCustomer: Rural health networks and FQHCs serving 2,000-15,000 patients with 1-5 clinic locations
valueProposition.uniqueAngle: Only platform that works reliably on rural internet while integrating seamlessly with small clinic workflows
valueProposition.whyNow: COVID proved telehealth viability, permanent reimbursement changes make it economically viable, and federal rural health funding creates budget availability

businessModel.revenueStrategy: Dual revenue: $2K/month SaaS per clinic + $15 per specialist consultation
businessModel.pricingFramework: $2K/month justified by replacing $8K/month locum specialist costs; $15/visit competitive with 20-30% platform fees charged by others

unitEconomics.estimatedCAC: $8,000 per clinic (12-18 month sales cycle, field sales required)
unitEconomics.estimatedLTV: $84,000 per clinic (assuming 3.5 year retention, $2K monthly + $500 monthly visit fees)

businessModel.scalabilityAssessment: Strong network effects as more specialists join platform. Technical infrastructure scales efficiently. Main constraint is field sales team growth and regulatory compliance across states.

technicalFeasibility.recommendedStack: React/Node.js frontend, Python/Django backend, WebRTC for video, AWS for HIPAA compliance

buildVsBuy.rationale: Use Twilio Video or Agora for HIPAA-compliant foundation, customize for bandwidth optimization
Your Epic background is competitive advantage, rural clinics need custom workflows
Use existing clearinghouse like Change Healthcare, too complex and regulated to build

technicalFeasibility.estimatedMvpTimeline: 6-8 months for basic platform, additional 3-4 months per major EHR integration

risks.description: Multi-state medical licensing, HIPAA compliance, and varying telehealth regulations create complex legal requirements
risks.mitigation: Start in single state, hire healthcare attorney, get HIPAA audit before launch

risks.description: Rural clinic adoption cycles are 12-24 months, delaying revenue and increasing cash burn
risks.mitigation: Focus on health networks vs individual clinics, leverage existing relationships from Epic background

risks.description: Teladoc or Amwell could add rural-specific features and leverage existing scale
risks.mitigation: Build deep rural clinic relationships and workflows that are hard to replicate

risks.description: Reimbursement rates for rural telehealth may decrease as COVID emergency measures end
risks.mitigation: Focus on high-value specialties, build direct-pay options, track policy changes closely

risks.description: Recruiting specialists willing to do rural telehealth at competitive rates
risks.mitigation: Partner with academic medical centers, offer flexible scheduling, focus on high-demand specialties

week1to4: Choose launch state (recommend Texas - large rural population, favorable telehealth laws)
Map target health networks in chosen state
Interview 10 rural clinic administrators about workflows
week5to8: Hire healthcare attorney for regulatory guidance
Identify 3 target health networks for pilot partnerships
week9to12: Complete basic EHR integration with Epic
Apply for additional SBIR Phase II funding

decisionAgenda.question: Which state to launch in first?

options.tradeoff: Large market, favorable laws vs high competition
Less competition, clear need vs smaller market size
Good health networks, moderate size vs more complex regulations

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Texas - largest rural population (3.6M), favorable telehealth laws, strong health network infrastructure, and your Epic background likely has connections there

decisionAgenda.question: Primary care or specialty focus for launch?

options.tradeoff: High demand, easier licensing vs limited market size
Larger market, easier clinic buy-in vs lower reimbursement rates
Visual specialty fits telehealth, good reimbursement vs smaller provider pool

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Mental health - highest unmet need in rural areas, best reimbursement rates, and specialists more willing to do telehealth. Expand to primary care in year 2

decisionAgenda.question: Direct clinic sales vs health network partnerships?

options.tradeoff: Higher margins, direct relationships vs longer sales cycles
Faster scaling, shared risk vs lower margins, less control

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Start with 2-3 health network partnerships to prove model, then add direct clinic sales channel in year 2
